mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending proxies and driving immediate positive equity returns as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 within a 30-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and surges in housing starts indicate resource reallocation toward construction and supply chain bottlenecks, which compress corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 50% of out-of-sample rolling windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances alongside persistently low policy rates signal accommodative financial conditions that fuel equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative when RSAFS growth exceeds 10% annually while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, indicating liquidity saturation rather than risk-on behavior.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical 2.5 percent trend rate expands aggregate corporate revenue expectations, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarterly GDP beats are followed by negative 60-day equity returns in more than 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 500,000 signals accelerating labor demand, which rapidly improves near-term earnings outlooks and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 45% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial output and acceleration in the consumer price index reflects cost-push inflation that erodes real margins, forcing equity multiples to contract and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when INDPRO falls more than 1% and CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.5% month-over-month, or the interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of forward return variance.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.25 percent while core inflation expectations remain elevated, markets price in premature tightening that raises discount rates faster than earnings can grow, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in over 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25% and CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% annualized, indicating earnings growth successfully offsets rate hikes.
